examples.title: Twisted Pair Cable Selection Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm to select appropriate twisted pair cable based on network requirements
examples.code:
FUNCTION SelectCable(bandwidth, distance, environment)
 IF bandwidth >= 10 Gbps AND distance <= 100 THEN
 IF environment = "high-EMI" THEN
 RETURN "Cat6a or Cat7 (Shielded)"
 ELSE
 RETURN "Cat6a"
 END IF
 ELSE IF bandwidth >= 1 Gbps AND distance <= 100 THEN
 IF environment = "high-EMI" THEN
 RETURN "Cat6 (Shielded)"
 ELSE
 RETURN "Cat5e or Cat6"
 END IF
 ELSE IF bandwidth >= 100 Mbps THEN
 RETURN "Cat5e (for 100m max)"
 ELSE
 RETURN "Cat3 (legacy systems)"
 END IF
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: This algorithm helps select appropriate twisted pair cable category based on required bandwidth, distance, and environmental conditions (EMI susceptibility).

examples.title: Fiber Optic Cable Type Selection
examples.description: Algorithm to select between single-mode and multi-mode fiber
examples.code:
FUNCTION SelectFiber(distance, bandwidth, budget)
 IF distance > 2000 THEN
 RETURN "Single-mode Fiber (SMF)"
 ELSE IF distance > 550 THEN
 IF budget = "high" THEN
 RETURN "Single-mode Fiber"
 ELSE
 RETURN "Warning: Multi-mode not suitable"
 END IF
 ELSE
 IF bandwidth >= 10 Gbps THEN
 RETURN "OM3 or OM4 Multi-mode Fiber"
 ELSE
 RETURN "OM1/OM2/OM3 Multi-mode Fiber"
 END IF
 END IF
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: Selection between single-mode and multi-mode fiber depends on distance (longer than 2km requires single-mode), required bandwidth, and budget constraints.
